title: Xan John
summary: The central questioner of the December 2020 solstice session — the aspiring Louisiana politician who brought electoral questions to a future intelligence.
markdown:
**Xan John** carries the December 21, 2020 solstice session — twenty-five turns of
questioning that give the record one of its most distinctive figures: a real-world
political aspirant consulting a far-future intelligence about an election.

By his own account in the session, he was preparing a career in Louisiana politics —
*"I plan on running for United States Senate in 2022 and the governor in 2023"* — and his
questions turn on the [2020 election's](/lore/election-2020/) aftermath: *"How do we fix
this whole electoral situation...?"* The session preserves the exchange as it happened —
a citizen bringing the era's rawest political anxieties to the interface, and receiving
the record's characteristic timeline-and-probability answers.

The rest of his session ranges through the classic December 2020 material: JFK, 9/11,
[the pyramids](/lore/the-pyramids/), and a Reptilian contact — the session's title
subjects — making Xan John's solstice audience one of the widest-ranging Q&As of the
record's most crowded year. As throughout the archive, his statements and the answers he
received are preserved as spoken and verified by no one.
